title: Computer Basics & Office Skills Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of hardware, operating systems, MS Office, the internet, and data science fundamentals.

questions.question: What does CPU stand for?
Which type of storage retains data without power (non-volatile)?
In Microsoft Excel, which function calculates the average of a range?
What does HTTP stand for?
options: B. Hyperlink and Text Transition Protocol
C. Host Transfer and Tunneling Protocol

questions.question: What is the primary job of an Operating System?
options: A. Manage hardware resources and provide services to applications

questions.question: In Excel, which shortcut autofills a selected cell formula down a column?
What is RAM used for?
options: A. Permanent storage of the OS
C. Temporary fast storage for actively running programs

questions.question: Which protocol secures web traffic by encrypting it?
In Python's pandas, what does df.head() return?
options: D. The data types of each column

questions.question: What is the function of a router on a network?
options: A. Connect devices within the same LAN by MAC address
B. Forward packets between networks based on IP address

questions.question: In Word, which shortcut adds a page break?
What does SSD stand for?
What Python library is primarily used for numerical array computations?
In PowerPoint, what is a 'Slide Master'?
options: A. The first slide in a presentation
B. A template that controls the design of all slides
C. The slide with the most content
D. A locked slide that cannot be edited

questions.question: What is an IP address?
options: B. A type of network cable
C. A numerical label assigned to each device on a network

questions.question: What does DNS resolve?
options: A. MAC addresses to IP addresses
B. IP addresses to physical locations
D. Domain names to IP addresses

questions.question: Which Excel function looks up a value in a table by row?
What is 'virtualization' in computing?
options: B. Running multiple virtual machines on one physical host
C. Backing up data to the cloud

questions.question: In data science, what is a 'DataFrame' in pandas?
options: C. A 2D tabular data structure with labeled axes
D. A Python function for data cleaning

questions.question: What does the 'motherboard' do in a computer?
options: D. Connects and allows communication between all hardware components

questions.question: In Word, 'Track Changes' is used to:
options: A. Record edits made so reviewers can see and accept/reject them
C. Sync documents to the cloud automatically

questions.question: What is a 'cookie' in web browsing?
options: B. A small data file stored by a browser to remember session info
D. A type of web server

questions.question: Which pandas method removes rows with missing values?
What does 'cloud computing' mean?
options: B. Processing data inside a physical cloud server room only
D. Delivering computing services (servers, storage, software) over the internet

questions.question: In Excel, what does the '$' symbol do in a cell reference like '$A$1'?
options: A. Makes the reference absolute so it does not change when the formula is copied
B. Formats the cell as currency
C. Refers to a named range
D. Calculates the sum of column A
